Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD only performs standard operations in its top-level scope and `pkgver()`: variable assignments, source array definitions (all `git+https` pointing to the official `psi-im` GitHub repositories), and a `pkgver()` function that reads a version file and runs `git rev-list`/`git rev-parse` for versioning. No eval, no hidden network requests, no obfuscated code, and no dangerous commands are executed during sourcing or `pkgver()`. The `prepare()`, `build()`, and `package()` functions contain routine build/install logic but are not executed by the two targeted commands (`--noprepare`, `--nobuild`). All checksums are SKIP, which is expected for VCS sources. No evidence of malicious behavior exists in the scope that will be run.
</details>
<summary>Standard PKGBUILD with safe top-level and pkgver() logic.</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- Standard PKGBUILD with safe top-level and pkgver() logic.[/]

audits.body:
Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/psi-plus-git.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-tj5un9n7...
Cloned repository to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-tj5un9n7
Analyzing psi-plus-git AUR package (working in /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-tj5un9n7) with qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines three Git repositories in the `source` array, all pointing to legitimate upstream locations under the `psi-im` organization. The `sha256sums` are set to 'SKIP' as expected for VCS sources. During `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`, the PKGBUILD is sourced, which executes the `pkgver()` function.

The `pkgver()` function changes into the `psi` directory and reads a version file, then uses `git rev-list` and `git rev-parse` to compute a detailed revision string. These are standard Git operations that do not involve network communication or code execution beyond inspecting the local state of the already-checked-out repository. No dangerous command substitution, obfuscated code, or remote code execution is present in the global scope or in `pkgver()`.

While `prepare()` contains `git submodule update` with configuration manipulation (`protocol.file.allow=always`), this function is **not executed** due to `--noprepare`. Therefore, it is out of scope for this narrow gate. Only the sourcing of the file and `pkgver()` execution are in scope — both are safe here.

Thus, running the specified `makepkg` commands poses no risk of executing malicious code.
</details>
<summary>
Sourcing PKGBUILD and fetching sources is safe
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- Sourcing PKGBUILD and fetching sources is safe[/]

Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/psi-plus-git.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/anthropic-claude-sonnet-4.6/aur-sleuth-6mbtc6kp...

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
**Source array:** All three sources are fetched via `git+https://github.com/psi-im/` — the legitimate upstream organization for the Psi XMPP client. No unexpected or unrelated hosts are used. Checksums are set to `SKIP`, which is the correct and required practice for VCS sources.

**Top-level scope:** No suspicious commands execute at the global/top-level scope of the PKGBUILD.

**`pkgver()` function:** Reads `${srcdir}/psi/version` (a file from the already-cloned upstream repo) and runs standard `git rev-list`/`git rev-parse` commands to construct a version string. No network requests, no shell eval, no data exfiltration — this is a textbook `pkgver()` implementation for a `-git` package.

`prepare()`, `build()`, and `package()` are out of scope for this gate and will be reviewed separately.
</details>
<summary>Sources from legitimate upstream; pkgver() is standard and safe.</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- Sources from legitimate upstream; pkgver() is standard and safe.[/]

data.reasoning: Both models confirmed PKGBUILD's safety in executed scopes (top-level and pkgver()). No dangerous operations were found in code paths triggered by 'makepkg --nobuild'. Failed source download prevents full verification but doesn't indicate malice.
coverage_issues: Failed source download prevented review of build scripts, .install files, and submodules referenced in prepare()
false_negatives: Potential risks in prepare()'s git submodule update (not reviewed due to --noprepare and failed download)
learnings: Handle source download failures more gracefully to review critical build files
Prioritize review of package hooks (.install) even when sources are partially unavailable
Improve error handling to distinguish between benign VCS issues and true security risks
